input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

Every property that needs to change gets its own `AppThemeBinding` with a `Light` and `Dark` value. The framework switches automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent — `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` is the code-behind equivalent of `AppThemeBinding`. It reacts to theme changes at runtime the same way.

---

**Tips:**
- For reuse, define themed colors in your `App.xaml` resource dictionary and reference them by key instead of inline hex values.
- To detect or override the theme manually: `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` returns the current theme; setting `Application.Current.UserAppTheme` forces one.
